$comment: DESIGN.md §20.3.1 point 6: a manifest may exclude a column/field from comparison for a specific pass only by citing a §21 caveat id. A58 (§11.6) NARROWED this exemption. It used to apply to all three passes, because C10 made step-into refuse dynamic SQL, so every pass stepped over the EXEC(@s) and re-materialized (§10.7). The Into pass now PUSHES a dynamic frame and is EXACT -- the same shape as p21, this fixture's procedure-callee twin -- so the exemption is scoped to over/boost via the optional 'passes' array (the p15_ext/p21 convention).

exemptions.reason: On the OVER and BOOST passes the dynamic child batch is an indirect ERROR_*() consumer reached from an active error context, so §10.7 re-materializes that context around the stepped-over EXEC(@s) -- and re-materialization raises a NEW error, so ERROR_NUMBER() inside the child reads RAISERROR's own 50000. Expected: native=8134 (fact 7: dynamic SQL invoked from a CATCH block sees the real error), debugger(over/boost)=50000. The INTO pass is NOT exempt: A58 pushes a dynamic frame, R7 substitution reaches the ERROR_*() references inside the dynamic text, and the shadow serves the true 8134 -- which is exactly C21's own prescribed remedy ('step into the callee for exact per-statement values'), unavailable for dynamic SQL until A58. See DESIGN.md §21 rows C21 and C10.
